behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive directory event delivery + path handoff), not a single function. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE are both yes: in-process event fakes can miss OS-specific recursive watch gaps. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly depends on actual placement in a real subdirectory under the watched root. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML step exists. MATRIX-USAGE: Integration vs System row settled this.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon process pointed at temp input/output dirs. Create a nested subdirectory under input and write a file there. Assert one transformed output appears for that nested file path without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets local dedup rule correctness for same path + time window. MOCK-MISS says minimum boundary is one object/function (path key + window check), not OS interaction. Rejected Integration because behavior text is specifically "deduplication logic" and does not require watcher/process wiring to manifest. System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified: no real infra or AI dependency is required for this failure mode. MATRIX-USAGE: Unit vs Integration row settled this.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate dedup component with a known window and fake clock. Submit request for path P twice within window and once after window. Assert outcomes: accept, reject, accept.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS multi-event emission; the failure appears at real watcher/OS boundary. REAL-INFRA is yes, and MOCK-HIDE is yes because synthetic single-event mocks often miss duplicate native event bursts. Rejected Integration due concrete behavior text: "operating system emits multiple filesystem events". Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model decision exists. MATRIX-USAGE: Integration vs System row settled this.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real temp directory. Perform one write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+rename or chunked save) and capture processing count for that file. Assert exactly one pipeline execution/output.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS filesystem lifecycle behavior (delete/recreate inode/path) and long-running watch reattachment. REAL-INFRA is required; in-process mocks can hide missed re-subscription edge cases. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires directory removal/recreation while process runs. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified (no AI/ML). MATRIX-USAGE: Integration vs System row settled this.
behaviors.test_description: Launch daemon watching a real directory. Delete watched dir, recreate it at same path, then add a new file. Assert daemon processes post-recreation file without restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering correctness depends on real arrival concurrency plus filesystem timestamp semantics/granularity and stat retrieval under actual I/O timing. Mocks can force idealized timestamps and hide real ordering failures. Rejected Integration because behavior text ties order to real file creation timestamps at arrival time, an OS/filesystem boundary concern. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified (no AI/ML). MATRIX-USAGE: Integration vs System row settled this.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real temp dirs. Create several files near-simultaneously with controlled creation order, then observe recorded processing sequence. Assert sequence matches creation timestamp ordering rule (including defined tie-breaker if equal).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode depends on real write-in-progress timing, watcher event timing, and file-read readiness on OS/filesystem. REAL-INFRA is yes; fake writers/events usually hide partial-write races. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires handling an arrival event during an active write. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified (no AI/ML). MATRIX-USAGE: Integration vs System row settled this.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon and begin writing a large file in chunks with delays so arrival event fires before final chunk. Verify daemon does not process intermediate partial content and processes only after writer closes/completes.
